objective: Define optional, safe provider probes so trusted workers can test whether configured API keys appear to work without exposing raw secret values.

probes.plainEnglish: Checks whether OpenAI accepts the API key.
probes.ifFail: OpenAI-powered chat, summaries, or memory features may not work.

probes.plainEnglish: Checks whether Gemini accepts the API key.
probes.ifFail: Gemini-powered chat, voice, or analysis features may not work.

probes.plainEnglish: JWT secrets cannot be externally validated against a provider. They should be validated through local auth flows.
probes.ifFail: Login and session handling may fail or be insecure if the secret is weak or missing.

probes.plainEnglish: Database credentials should be validated through a connection check in the app backend, not a generic public probe.
probes.ifFail: Features that need saved data may fail.

probes.plainEnglish: Webhook verification tokens should be validated through the integration flow, not a generic external probe.

conclusion: Trusted workers should only run external probes when they can do so without revealing raw secrets, and they should explain outcomes in plain language with choices and consequences.
